name: Stuffed Venison Pinwhells
recipeIngredient: 1 can Rotel tomatoes with diced chiles
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the backstrap fillet by cutting it perpendicular to your body and making a shallow slice about 0.25 inches deep around the perimeter until the backstrap comes out looking like a round steak.
2. Place both pieces into a container and cover with whole milk. Marinate for at least 6 hours, or overnight.
3. Remove meat from milk, pat dry with paper towels, and sprinkle with wild game seasoning, rubbing it into the meat.
4. Preheat oven to 400°F.
5. In a food processor, mix Velveeta cheese and Rotel tomatoes until smooth and creamy.
6. Spread thin layer of cheese mixture over one side of deer fillet. Place bacon strips on top, followed by green onions and minced garlic.
7. Roll up the flattened fillets tightly and set aside.
8. Lay out 8-10 bacon strips on a work surface and place a rolled backstrap on top. Wrap bacon around venison and secure with toothpicks.
9. Repeat with second backstrap.
10. Slice rolled venison into 2-inch thick pinwheels, arrange on a baking sheet, and bake uncovered for 40-45 minutes. Baste with sauce from the bottom of the pan as needed. Avoid overcooking to ensure tenderness and juiciness.
